P5_self_verify_post_verdict.incident: Case-insensitive rm pitfall — 3 critical canon files accidentally deleted during recovery from `detect-case-dup-pairs.sh` false-positive output
P5_self_verify_post_verdict.root_cause: macOS HFS+ case-insensitive filesystem: `rm lowercase` silently deleted the uppercase twin (same inode). The bundled detection script also had a bug: `[ -f "$lower" ]` always returned true (same inode lookup), so it reported phantom pairs even when only one physical file existed.
P5_self_verify_post_verdict.fix: 1) Patched `~/.hermes/skills/autonomous-change-pipeline/scripts/detect-case-dup-pairs.sh` to use inode comparison (skip if `stat -f%i upper == stat -f%i lower`). 2) Resynced patched script to GitHub mirror at `~/Projects/BossMan/hermes/skills/autonomous-change-pipeline/scripts/detect-case-dup-pairs.sh`. 3) Recovered 3 critical files (SERVICES_MAP.md, BLOCKER_RESOLUTIONS.md, PHASEREPORT_DOC_FIXES_2026-06-23.md) from BossMan/docs/ + regenerated report from P1 data.
P5_self_verify_post_verdict.verified_post_fix: Detection script now reports 0 pairs (exit 0). All 3 critical files md5-aligned across Hermes canon + Obsidian + BossMan/docs. Patched script + file recovery confirmed.
P5_self_verify_post_verdict.lesson_saved_to: memory: macOS case-dup pitfall (rm + detection script false-positive). skill: autonomous-change-pipeline case-dup section updated.

signed_off_by: BossMan (autonomous remediation model, no carve-outs triggered)
